target.name: H2Pro
target.founder: Gideon Grader, Avner Rothschild, Hen Dotan, Talmon Marco

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
H2Pro is a Series B-stage frontier technology company in the Climate & Energy sector, with $100M+ in total capital raised to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe comprising 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private entities, including KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, Amogy, Helion, and Redwood Materials—all operating in adjacent energy transition and advanced materials domains.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses $100M of total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-subsequent-stage step-ups, implying a $190M valuation. The peer-median approach identifies three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: KoBold Metals ($3.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C). The median across this set is $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $3.0B.

Triangulating these methods yields a valuation range of $162M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $595M. The wide spread reflects the heterogeneity of peer stage progression and business maturity within the climate-tech investment landscape. Key uncertainties include the specific technology readiness level and commercialization timeline of H2Pro's core offering relative to Series C-stage peers, and the degree to which downstream capital intensity will compress or expand near-term return multiples.
